subject: Limit Theorems
question: Let $X$ be a random variable. Show the following (a) $E(X^2) \ge [E(X)]^2$. (b) If $X \ge 0$ then $E\left(\frac{1}{X}\right) \ge \frac{1}{E(X)}$. (c) If $X > 0$ then $-E[\ln X] \ge -\ln[E(X)]$
